last_updated: 2026-05-06 +5 effect Legos (PopBurst/ScoreRise/BoardShake/SpawnDrop/HintPulse) + NL keyword expansion on chess/match3/asteroids/pong
notes: Catalog for reusable SwiftUI/AppKit macOS Lego components. AIWizard should copy canonical Swift source files into generated macOSApp artifacts before writing app-specific glue.

categories.description: Reusable engine, dictionary, keyboard, and smoke-test helpers for native five-letter word guessing games.
Reusable mini-crossword grid, clue, entry, selection, and validation helpers.
Reusable grid, reveal, match-three, Mastermind, and puzzle-state engines.
Reusable 8x8 board, checkers, and chess rule engines for native SwiftUI board games.
Reusable model engines for Asteroids, Pong, Adventure, Frogger, PacMan-style mazes, platformers, and tower defense.
Reusable card, deck, blackjack, and poker hand evaluation engines.
Reusable piano note and keyboard state helpers for native music toys.

Reusable macOS app shell and scene affordances. Provides MacOSMiniHeader for compact title bars (<=44pt regular, <=32pt narrow) so generated apps stop wasting screen real estate on oversized hero blocks.
Reusable SpriteKit scene, input, HUD, and game-loop helpers. Planned after the shell layer is stable.

Cross-cutting visual + audio effect tuning structs. Provides AnimationIntensity (subtle/standard/punchy presets for squash-stretch, bulge-shrink, fall springs, flash overlays). Game-flavor reference apps reference these instead of hardcoding magic numbers — generators pick a preset based on game pace.

components.name: WordGameEngine
components.summary: Observable five-letter word guessing engine with duplicate-letter scoring, row advancement, permissive fallback validation, and reset support.

contracts.input: Call inputLetter(_:), deleteLetter(), and submitGuess() from physical or on-screen controls.
contracts.state: Bind rows, currentInput, state, and statusMessage into SwiftUI views.
contracts.validation: Default dictionary allows known answers plus permissive five-letter ASCII fallback so Enter never looks dead.

components.name: WordGameLexicon
components.summary: Inline curated five-letter answer and valid-guess lists that avoid Bundle.module resource pitfalls.

components.name: WordGameKeyboardHandling
components.summary: SwiftUI ViewModifier for focused physical keyboard input using stable onKeyPress signatures for letters, Return, and Delete.

contracts.return: Uses .onKeyPress(.return, phases: .down) with explicit closure parameter.
contracts.delete: Uses .onKeyPress(.delete, phases: .down) with explicit closure parameter.

components.name: WordGameSmokeTests
components.summary: Runtime smoke-test reporter that exercises a wrong guess, reset, and winning guess so the Harness sees useful stdout.

contracts.coverage: Exercises row advancement, reset, and win path without UI automation.

components.name: CrosswordEngine
components.summary: Mini-crossword state engine with numbered entries, across/down selection, letter input, clue lookup, and solved validation.

contracts.state: Bind grid, selectedPoint, selectedDirection, entries, and isSolved into SwiftUI.
contracts.input: Call select(_:direction:), input(_:), and delete() from pointer and keyboard controls.

components.name: MiniCrosswordBank
components.summary: Curated bank of 10 NYT-mini-style crossword puzzles with daily rotation. MiniCrosswordSeed.daily() routes through this bank; the View can also use puzzle(at:) for prev/next navigation and dailyIndex() for a 'Puzzle X of N' HUD.

contracts.rotation: Use MiniCrosswordBank.daily(referenceDate:) for the daily puzzle. Use MiniCrosswordBank.dailyIndex() for the (index, total) tuple to render 'Puzzle X of N'.
contracts.navigation: Use MiniCrosswordBank.puzzle(at:) for prev/next chevron navigation.

goodFits: NYT mini clone with prev/next navigation

components.name: CrosswordSmokeTests
components.summary: Runtime smoke-test reporter for mini-crossword entry input and solved-state wiring.

components.name: GridPuzzleCore
components.summary: Generic Codable grid, coordinate, direction, neighbor, and deterministic RNG primitives shared by puzzle and board engines.

contracts.coordinates: Use PuzzlePoint(row:col:) and GridDirection for deterministic row/column movement.
contracts.grid: PuzzleGrid stores Codable/Equatable cells and provides contains, neighbors, and allPoints helpers.

components.name: MastermindEngine
components.summary: Mastermind code-breaker engine with exact/color-only peg scoring, turn history, and completion state.

contracts.feedback: Use MastermindEngine.evaluate(secret:guess:) for duplicate-safe exact and color-only scoring.
contracts.turns: Call submit(_:) once per guess and render guesses plus feedback pegs from guesses.

components.name: MastermindSmokeTests
components.summary: Runtime smoke-test reporter for Mastermind scoring and duplicate-safe feedback without requiring other grid-puzzle engines.

components.name: MinesweeperEngine
components.summary: Minesweeper engine with mine seeding, adjacency counts, flagging, reveal, flood reveal, win, and loss state.

contracts.input: Call reveal(at:) for clicks and toggleFlag(at:) for secondary-click or flag mode.
contracts.state: Render each MinesweeperCell from grid and end the game from state.

components.name: MinesweeperSmokeTests
components.summary: Runtime smoke-test reporter for Minesweeper reveal, flag, and state wiring without requiring other grid-puzzle engines.

components.name: Match3Engine
components.summary: Match-three engine with adjacent swapping, run detection, clearing, column collapse, refill, cascades, and scoring.

contracts.input: Call swap(_: _:) only for adjacent selected tiles; false means rollback/no valid match.
contracts.rendering: Render grid symbols with app-specific candy/tile visuals.

components.name: Match3SmokeTests
components.summary: Runtime smoke-test reporter for Match3 board setup, playable swap discovery, cascade resolution, and scoring without requiring other grid-puzzle engines.

components.name: GridPuzzleSmokeTests
components.summary: Runtime smoke-test reporter for Mastermind, Minesweeper, and Match3 engines.

components.name: BoardGameCore
components.summary: Codable 8x8 board, pieces, sides, moves, algebraic coordinates, and piece iteration shared by chess and checkers.

contracts.coordinates: Use Board8x8.algebraic(_:) and Board8x8.squareName(_:) for chess/checkers notation.
contracts.state: Board8x8 stores optional BoardPiece values by PuzzlePoint.

components.name: CheckersRules
components.summary: Checkers setup, diagonal slide/capture generation, forced-capture preference, kinging, and move application.

contracts.moves: Use allLegalMoves(for:on:) to enforce forced captures before rendering selectable moves.
contracts.apply: Call apply(_:to:) after the player chooses a legal move.

components.name: ChessRules
components.summary: Chess setup, normal piece movement, captures, pawn promotion, self-check filtering, check, and checkmate helpers.

contracts.scope: Covers normal legal moves and promotion; castling and en passant can be layered in app-specific glue.
contracts.moves: Use legalMoves(from:on:) for selected-piece highlighting and allLegalMoves(for:on:) for game-over checks.

components.name: BoardGameSmokeTests
components.summary: Runtime smoke-test reporter for shared Board8x8 coordinate, piece, and iteration primitives.

components.name: ArcadeCore
components.summary: Shared vector, body, playfield, control, phase, integration, wrapping, and circle-intersection primitives for native arcade engines.

contracts.loop: Call update(dt:controls:) from a SwiftUI TimelineView, Timer, or display-link bridge.
contracts.rendering: Render ArcadeBody positions with SwiftUI Canvas, shapes, or SpriteKit glue.

components.name: AsteroidsEngine
components.summary: Asteroids-style engine with rotating/thrusting ship, shots, wrapping rocks, splitting, score, lives, waves, and collision state.

contracts.input: Call update(dt:controls:) for rotation/thrust and fire() from keyboard or toolbar actions.
contracts.state: Render ship, rocks, shots, score, lives, and phase from the engine.

components.name: PongEngine
components.summary: Pong engine with ball/paddle movement, wall bounces, paddle bounces, AI paddle fallback, scoring, and win state.

contracts.input: Call update(dt:leftDirection:rightDirection:) and pass -1/0/1 from keyboard controls.
contracts.state: Render ball, paddles, score, winningScore, and phase.

components.name: GridAdventureEngine
components.summary: Tile-map adventure engine for Atari Adventure, Frogger lanes, and PacMan-style pellet mazes with hero movement, collectibles, exits, enemies, vehicles, Frogger logs, score, lives, respawn, and phase.

contracts.maps: Use adventureCastleMap(), froggerLaneMap(), or mazePelletMap() as seed layouts and customize tiles in app glue.
contracts.input: Call moveHero(_:) for keyboard movement and stepTraffic() for lane/enemy ticks.

components.name: PlatformerEngine
components.summary: Side-scrolling platformer engine with acceleration, gravity, jumping, grounded state, platforms, simple coin scoring, and loss state.

contracts.rendering: Use player.body, platforms, coins, score, and phase to draw SwiftUI or SpriteKit scenes.

components.name: TowerDefenseEngine
components.summary: Tower-defense model with 4 turret kinds (basic/cannon/frost/beam — mirroring web `entities/TurretVariants.js`), 3 enemy kinds (normal/fast/heavy), splash damage, frost-slow, range targeting, cooldowns, waves, credits, lives, and win/loss phase. Emits ArcadeProjectile records on every fire so renderers can draw tracers, cannon shells, frost pulses, and continuous beams.

contracts.input: Call placeTower(at:kind:) from grid clicks and startWave(count:) from a wave button.
contracts.loop: Call update(dt:) from the app timer; render `engine.enemies` AND `engine.projectiles` each frame.

goodFits: frost slows + cannon AoE + laser beams

components.name: ArcadeProjectile
components.summary: Visual record of an in-flight or beam-active shot. Mirrors web `entities/Projectile.js` plus the beam-draw mode from `entities/TurretVariants.js`. Tower-defense engines append instances on fire; renderers read `engine.projectiles` each frame and draw tracers/cannon-shells/frost-pulses/beams accordingly.

contracts.state: Travel-class kinds use `t` (0..1) over `lifetime`; beam kinds have `lifetime=0` and rely on the parent tower's `lastTargetID` to stay alive.
contracts.rendering: Renderer should fade or clear travel-class projectiles when `t >= 1` and stop drawing beam-class when `live == false`.

components.name: ArcadeSmokeTests
components.summary: Runtime smoke-test reporter for shared arcade core primitives; app glue should add recipe-specific gameplay smoke lines.

components.name: ArcadeSmokeTestsPacman
components.summary: Pacman maze-connectivity BFS smoke test. Requires gridpuzzle.core (PuzzlePoint) AND grid-adventure.core (GridAdventureEngine.pacmanMapForLevel). Only ship in Pacman-flavored recipes.

contracts.stdout: Call ArcadeSmokeTestsPacman.printPacmanConnectivityReport() from app init() in Pacman-flavored apps. Verifies all 5 layouts have connected floors.
contracts.depends-on: REQUIRES gridpuzzle.core (for PuzzlePoint) and grid-adventure.core (for GridAdventureEngine.pacmanMapForLevel) to be selected in the recipe.

components.name: CardGameCore
components.summary: Standard card, suit, rank, deterministic deck, draw, shuffle, and blackjack hand-value primitives.

contracts.deck: Use CardDeck.draw(_:) and reset by constructing a new CardDeck when low.

components.name: BlackjackEngine
components.summary: Blackjack table engine with deal, hit, stand, dealer policy, settlement, bankroll, bet, phase, and status text.

contracts.flow: Call deal(bet:), hit(), and stand(); render player/dealer hands, bankroll, bet, phase, and status.

components.name: PokerHandEvaluator
components.summary: Five-to-seven-card poker evaluator with hand ranks, kickers, comparison, straights, flushes, full houses, and straight flushes.

contracts.evaluation: Call PokerHandEvaluator.evaluate(_:) with five to seven cards.
contracts.scope: Provides hand ranking; betting, pots, and AI opponents belong in app-specific glue.

components.name: CardGameSmokeTests
components.summary: Runtime smoke-test reporter for shared card deck, draw, and blackjack hand-value primitives.

components.name: PianoEngine
components.summary: Piano keyboard model with note names, MIDI numbers, frequencies, sharp-key metadata, and active note state.

contracts.input: Call noteOn(_:) and noteOff(_:) from pointer or keyboard bindings.
contracts.audio: Use PianoNote.frequency to drive AVAudioEngine or NSSound glue in the generated app.

components.name: SpriteKitPhysicsHelpers
components.summary: Canonical SpriteKit physics body factories + Swift 6 concurrency-safe SKPhysicsContact snapshot pattern. Replaces hallucinated SKPhysicsBody(ellipseOf:) and provides nonisolated didBegin → @MainActor Task hop without sending non-Sendable contact data across the boundary.

contracts.physics-body: Use SKPhysicsHelpers.makeCircularBody/makeRectangularBody/makeOvalBody/makeStaticBody/makeSceneBoundary instead of constructing SKPhysicsBody directly. SKPhysicsBody(ellipseOf:) DOES NOT EXIST — use circleOfRadius/rectangleOf/polygonFrom instead (mac-017).
contracts.contact-handler: In nonisolated didBegin(_ contact:), call SKPhysicsHelpers.snapshot(of: contact) BEFORE entering Task { @MainActor in ... }. The snapshot is Sendable; SKPhysicsContact itself is not, so it cannot cross the actor boundary directly (mac-019).
contracts.categories: Use SKArcadeCategory.player1/.player2/.enemy/.projectile/.platform/.lava/.pickup/.goal/.wall as your default bitmask values; extend if you need more.

components.name: AnimationIntensity
components.summary: Game-flavor animation tuning struct. Composes squash/stretch amounts, animation durations, spring params, and flash overlay scale/opacity into one struct with .subtle / .standard / .punchy presets. Switch the preset to rescale every effect together; override individual knobs for fine-tuning.

contracts.input: Game view declares `private let intensity: AnimationIntensity = .standard` (or .subtle / .punchy).
contracts.output: Struct fields drive scaleEffect amounts, .animation durations, spring response/damping, flash overlay font-size multiplier, opacity, halo radius.

useCases: Match-3 games (sparkle/burst/lightning flash sizing + bulge-shrink-squash tuning)
Tile-based puzzle games (subtle preset for productivity-flavored apps)
Arcade games (punchy preset for action feedback)
Any game with squash-stretch tile animations that needs to be re-tuned without hunting magic numbers

components.name: SoundEngine
components.summary: Game-flavor sound effects engine. Loads CC0-licensed Kenney SFX bundled in `assets/sfx/`. Pooled AVAudioPlayer instances for low-latency overlap, debounced playback, music fade in/out, persistent UserDefaults mute, intensity preset matching AnimationIntensity. SFX referenced by semantic enum case (SFX.match3Pop, SFX.arcadeExplosion, etc.) so the underlying audio file can change without touching every game.

contracts.input: Game view declares `@ObservedObject private var sound = SoundEngine.shared`. Plays via `sound.play(.match3Pop)`, `sound.play(.match3Pop, pitchSemitones: 4)`, `sound.loop(.puzzleAmbient, fadeIn: 1.5)`, `sound.stopMusic(fadeOut: 1.0)`. Mute toggle: `sound.muted.toggle()`.
contracts.output: Asynchronously plays via AVAudioPlayer pool. Honors mute, debounce window, intensity volume scaling. Music persists across UI updates; SFX are one-shot.

useCases: Match-3 (pop on 3-match, burst on 4-match, bomb on 5-match, ascending pitch on cascades)
Arcade games (laser/explosion/thruster/shield/power-up/game-over)
Card games (slide/shove/dice-throw/chip-place)
Puzzle games (UI button-tap, error, toggle, victory chime)
ANY game needing sound. Productivity/utility apps OPT OUT — don't import SoundEngine.
assetDependencies: frameworks/macos-components/assets/sfx/*.m4a (25 CC0 SFX from Kenney UI/Casino/SciFi/Impact packs)
App must copy needed SFX into Contents/Resources/SFX/ at build time. SwiftPM: declare `resources: [.copy("SFX")]` on the target.

components.name: LevelManager
components.summary: Game-flavor level / progression manager. Encapsulates target-score curves (.casual/.standard/.hardcore/.accelerating/.custom), per-level resource budgets, suggested palette/spawn-rate/animation-intensity bumps, level-up celebration window (banner + SFX trigger), and persistent currentLevel via UserDefaults. Game-specific UI binds to currentLevel / currentTarget / celebratingLevelUp / celebrationProgress.

contracts.input: Game view declares `@StateObject private var levels = LevelManager(gameID: "<gameid>", baseTarget: 1500, baseMovesAllowed: 30, curve: .standard)`. UI reads levels.currentLevel / levels.currentTarget / levels.suggestedPaletteSize / levels.celebratingLevelUp / levels.celebrationProgress.
contracts.output: @Published currentLevel (1..maxLevel), currentTarget (Int), currentMovesAllowed (Int), celebrationProgress (CGFloat 0→1→0 over ~3s after advance()). Persists currentLevel under 'LevelManager.<gameID>.currentLevel' in UserDefaults.

useCases: Match-3 progression (target 1500 → 2100 → 2940 → ... at .standard 1.4× curve, palette 5 → 6 at L5 → 7 at L10 → 8 at L15)
Arcade games with waves (spawn-rate bumps via suggestedSpawnRateMultiplier)
Puzzle games with grid expansion (suggestedPaletteSize doubles as 'symbol set size')
Any game where animation intensity should bump as levels rise (suggestedAnimationIntensity returns 'subtle'/'standard'/'punchy')

components.name: HighScoreManager
components.summary: Personal-best score tracker. Persists best + last score per gameID via UserDefaults; exposes isAheadOfBest / deltaToBest / bestScoreDisplay helpers for live HUD readouts. commit(score:) returns true on a new best and fires a celebration window (celebratingNewBest + celebrationProgress). Multi-mode games use compound gameID (e.g. 'match3.easy' / 'match3.hard').

contracts.input: @StateObject private var highScores = HighScoreManager(gameID: "<game>"). UI reads bestScore, bestScoreDisplay, isAheadOfBest(currentScore:), deltaToBest(currentScore:), celebratingNewBest, celebrationProgress.
contracts.output: @Published bestScore (Int), lastScore (Int), celebratingNewBest (Bool), celebrationProgress (CGFloat 0→1→0 over ~2.5s after a new best). Persists 'HighScoreManager.<gameID>.bestScore' and '.lastScore' in UserDefaults.

useCases: Match-3 / arcade / puzzle games (BEST pill shows personal best, live delta vs current)
Multi-mode games (gameID 'asteroids.classic' vs 'asteroids.endless')
Score-attack games where the only goal is beating your own record

components.name: PopBurst
components.summary: Radial light flash for tile-clear / match-clear / explosion events. SwiftUI port of the web Lego of the same name. Drop-in for match-3, mahjong, jewel-puzzle, line-clear games. Apply as `.popBurst(trigger:)` modifier — each new non-nil trigger value spawns a one-shot flash overlay that scales out + fades. Per wisdom rule mac-028 (anim-feedback on every event) — every tile-clear needs a 'thing happened here' beat or the board reads as silent.

contracts.input: View modifier `.popBurst(trigger: T?, intensity: PopBurstIntensity = .normal, color: Color = .yellow)`. Trigger is any Equatable+Sendable; pass Date? or UUID? so each event reads as a unique value.
contracts.output: When trigger changes to a non-nil value, an overlay PopBurstView is spawned at view center, scales 0.4 → endScale (1.4/1.8/2.4 by intensity), opacity 0.95 → 0, removes via auto-fade in 0.42-0.62s.

useCases: Match-3 / Bejeweled / Candy Crush — each cleared tile gets a burst
Asteroids / Space shooter — each enemy hit gets a burst at the impact point
Breakout / brick clear — each broken brick gets a burst
Bubble Pop — each popped bubble gets a burst
Card games — milestone hand (royal flush, blackjack)

components.name: ScoreRise
components.summary: Floating '+50' / '+100' / '★ COMBO' popup that rises above a tile as points are awarded. SwiftUI port of the web Lego. Universal positive feedback for any score event in any genre. Apply as `.scoreRise(trigger: '+50')` modifier OR drop a ScoreRiseView at a board point. Pairs with score-format-with-thousands-separators wisdom for ≥1000.

contracts.input: View modifier `.scoreRise(trigger: String?, color: Color = .white, fontSize: CGFloat = 16)` or generic variant `.scoreRise(trigger: T?, textProvider: (T)->String)`. Trigger is any Equatable+Sendable.
contracts.output: When trigger changes to non-nil, ScoreRiseView text appears at view center, pops in (scale 0.6 → 1.0 in 0.18s), rises 42pt, fades to 0 over ~0.95s, auto-removes.

useCases: Match-3 — '+50' rises from cleared tile
Breakout — '+10' rises from broken brick
Idle clicker — '+1' rises from each tap
Quiz games — 'CORRECT! +25' on right answer
Combo events — 'COMBO ×3' in yellow at chain depth 3+

components.name: BoardShake
components.summary: Universal 'no-go / wrong answer / illegal move' shake. SwiftUI port of the web Lego. Applies a brief side-to-side jitter (with optional rotation on .strong) to any container. Per wisdom rule mac-028 — illegal/invalid input needs an immediate motion response or players can't tell whether it failed or the game is just slow.

contracts.input: View modifier `.boardShake(trigger: T?, intensity: BoardShakeIntensity = .normal)`. Trigger is any Equatable+Sendable.
contracts.output: When trigger changes, view shakes through a 5-step sequence over 0.25-0.52s by intensity (.subtle / .normal / .strong). Strong adds ±1° rotation. After completion, returns to identity transform.

useCases: Match-3 — invalid swap shake on the board
Chess / checkers — illegal move shake on the source square
Sudoku — wrong digit shake on the cell
Mastermind — wrong code shake on the guess row
Password / PIN — wrong code shake on the input

components.name: SpawnDrop
components.summary: Entry + exit micro-animations for tiles in any grid game. SwiftUI port of the web Lego. The view springs in from scale 0.2 with overshoot (matches the web's cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1)), and on `popping = true` bulges + rotates 8° + shrinks to 0. Caller removes the view from the hierarchy after the pop completes. Pairs with effects.pop-burst for the layered 'clear + flash' chain.

contracts.input: View modifier `.spawnDrop(popping: Bool, popDuration: Double = 0.28, spawnDuration: Double = 0.36)`. Spawn fires once on first appear; pop fires when `popping` flips false→true.
contracts.output: Entry: scale 0.2 → 1.0 with spring overshoot (1.12 peak). Exit: scale 1.0 → 1.35 + rotate 8° in popDuration*0.5, then scale 1.35 → 0 in popDuration*0.5.

useCases: Match-3 / Bejeweled — refilled tiles spawn-drop in
Tetris — spawned tetromino pulse
Mahjong — dealt tile entry
Solitaire — placed-card entry
Roguelikes — orb / pickup spawn

components.name: HintPulse
components.summary: Idle-aware hint glow for tile games. SwiftUI port of the web Lego. After idleSeconds of inactivity, surface a hint by toggling a glow on a small set of cells. Cancel on next interaction. Manual now() triggers an immediate hint that auto-fades after holdSeconds. Per wisdom rule game-idle-hint-pulse-macos — every tile puzzle reinvents this and gets corner cases wrong (forgetting to cancel on tap, leaking timers across game-over).

contracts.input: Construct `HintPulseController(idleSeconds: 6, holdSeconds: 2.4, findHint: { (vm: GameVM) -> [Coord] in ... })` then bind cells via `.hintPulse(active: vm.hints.isHinting(coord))`. Call `.schedule(self)` after each move; `.cancel()` on user interaction; `.now(self)` for explicit hint button; `.dispose()` on game-over.
contracts.output: After idleSeconds, controller calls findHint, populates `cells: Set<Coord>`. Bound cells pulse glow (scale 1.12 + yellow halo) on a 1.1s repeatForever animation until cancel() or hold timeout.

useCases: Match-3 / Candy Crush — hint at next valid swap after 6s idle
Sudoku — hint at next certain digit after idle
Mahjong — hint at next pair match after idle
Solitaire — hint at next legal move after idle
Puzzles — manual hint button

compositionRecipes.name: Native Word Game
assemblyNotes: Copy all selected component files verbatim under Sources/<TargetName>/MChatAIComponents/.
Keep app-specific UI in ContentView.swift or Views/*.swift; do not edit the copied component files.
Call WordGameSmokeTests.printLaunchReport() from the @main App init() after activation setup.
Use wordGameKeyboardHandling(onLetter:onDelete:onSubmit:) on the root playable surface so physical keyboard input works immediately.
Add visible ENTER and DELETE controls in the on-screen keyboard, even when physical keyboard handling is present.

compositionRecipes.name: Native Mini Crossword
assemblyNotes: Copy the crossword engine verbatim and build the SwiftUI board/clue list as app-specific glue.

Always include MiniCrosswordBank.swift — it has 10 curated puzzles + daily rotation + prev/next navigation. Use MiniCrosswordSeed.daily() for the initial load (which routes through the bank), and surface 'Puzzle X of N' + chevron prev/next in the View per pz-puzzle-bank-rotation wisdom.
Expose across/down selection, arrow-key movement, letter input, delete, active clue highlighting, and solved feedback.
Call CrosswordSmokeTests.printLaunchReport() from app init().

compositionRecipes.name: Native Mastermind
assemblyNotes: Use MastermindEngine for duplicate-safe scoring. Do not rewrite exact/color-only peg logic in ContentView.
Render selectable color pegs, guess history, feedback pegs, remaining turns, and win/loss state.
Call MastermindSmokeTests.printLaunchReport() from app init().

compositionRecipes.name: Native Minesweeper
assemblyNotes: Use MinesweeperEngine for mine placement, adjacency, flags, reveal, and flood reveal.
Add secondary-click flagging, visible flag mode, timer, difficulty controls, and win/loss overlays in app glue.
Call MinesweeperSmokeTests.printLaunchReport() from app init().

compositionRecipes.name: Native Match Three
assemblyNotes: Use Match3Engine for adjacent swaps, match detection, collapse, refill, cascades, and score.
Keep candy visuals, animations, combo labels, and level goals in app-specific SwiftUI glue.
Call Match3SmokeTests.printLaunchReport() from app init().

compositionRecipes.name: Native Asteroids / Top-Down Shooter
assemblyNotes: Use AsteroidsEngine for ship rotation, thrust, shots, wrapping, rock splitting, score, lives, and waves.
For Alien Abduction variants, keep the top-down controls and projectile loop but replace rocks with abductable targets in app glue.
Render with SwiftUI Canvas or SpriteKit; do not collapse the copied engine into a custom monolithic game loop.

compositionRecipes.name: Native Pong
assemblyNotes: Use PongEngine for ball physics, paddle movement, scoring, AI fallback, and win state.
Add keyboard shortcuts, pause/reset controls, score HUD, and optional sound in app glue.

compositionRecipes.name: Native Atari Adventure
assemblyNotes: Use GridAdventureEngine.adventureCastleMap() as the seed map, then add rooms, keys, doors, treasure, and enemy behavior in app glue.
Expose keyboard movement, inventory/status HUD, room labels, and clear win/loss feedback.

compositionRecipes.name: Native Frogger / Grid Arcade
assemblyNotes: Use GridAdventureEngine.froggerLaneMap() as the seed layout.
Keep lane data, moving hazards, safe zones, lives, and score in app glue while preserving the copied movement/collision engine.
Render safe logs on water and let stepTraffic() carry the hero when standing on AdventureActor.Kind.log.

compositionRecipes.name: Native PacMan / Maze Arcade
assemblyNotes: Use GridAdventureEngine.mazePelletMap() as the seed layout.
Render pellets, moving ghosts, score, lives, and reset controls in app-specific glue while preserving copied movement/collision rules.
Ghost chase/scatter rules and power pellets are polish layers; the base golden assembly should still be a playable pellet maze.

compositionRecipes.name: Native Platformer
assemblyNotes: Use PlatformerEngine for movement, gravity, jumping, platform collision, coins, score, and loss state.
Render levels, enemies, camera follow, HUD, and tile skins in app glue.

PlatformerEngine.coins is Set<PuzzlePoint>, where PuzzlePoint comes from gridpuzzle.core. GridPuzzleCore.swift is mandatory in the SwiftPM target — without it, Decodable synthesis on PlatformerEngine fails with 'cannot find type PuzzlePoint in scope'.

compositionRecipes.name: Native Tower Defense
assemblyNotes: Use TowerDefenseEngine for pathing, waves, 4 turret kinds (basic/cannon/frost/beam), targeting, cooldowns, frost-slow effect, splash damage, lives, credits, and phase.

Render `engine.enemies` AND `engine.projectiles` every frame in your SwiftUI Canvas/ZStack — tracers (basic) animate origin→target along `t`, cannon shells animate likewise plus an expanding splash ring on impact, frost pulses pulse blue at the impact tile, and beam projectiles draw a continuous glowing line from `originPoint` to the live position of the enemy referenced by `followsEnemyID`.

Surface a turret-shop toolbar (one button per TowerKind, dimmed when affordability fails) so the player picks BEFORE clicking a build tile. Mirrors `web-components/examples/tower-defense/index.html` UX.
Render a base marker at `path.last` (a shielded tile, lives = base HP) and a spawn marker at `path.first`.
Call ArcadeSmokeTests.printLaunchReport() from app init(), and add app-specific stdout confirming TowerDefenseEngine wiring.

compositionRecipes.name: Native Blackjack
assemblyNotes: Use BlackjackEngine for deal/hit/stand/dealer/settlement/bankroll behavior.
Generated glue should focus on card-table UI, betting controls, keyboard shortcuts, and status presentation.

compositionRecipes.name: Native Texas Holdem Poker
assemblyNotes: Use PokerHandEvaluator for hand ranking; app glue owns betting rounds, seats, pot UI, and opponent behavior.

For macOS prompts that mention Poker, prefer the native golden assembly shape: one human versus four CPU seats, dealer/blinds, chip stacks, pot, fold/check-call/raise/deal controls, hidden opponent hole cards, community flop/turn/river, logs, and tournament-style elimination toward heads-up play.

compositionRecipes.name: Native Checkers
assemblyNotes: Use CheckersRules for setup, legal moves, optional or mandatory capture policy, move application, and kinging.

Generated glue should handle board rendering, selected square, legal move highlights, turn prompts, restart, clear capture-policy feedback, and same-piece double-jump continuation after any chosen capture.

compositionRecipes.name: Native Chess
assemblyNotes: Use ChessRules for setup, legal move generation, move application, check, and checkmate.

Generated glue should render centered pieces and legal-target dots, selected square, captured status, promotion UI, reset, and a simple Black CPU opponent that replies automatically after White moves.

For macOS prompts that mention Chess, prefer the native golden assembly shape: an 8x8 board, rendered centered pieces, selected square, legal move targets, check/checkmate/stalemate status, captured pieces, reset, move log, and one-player White-versus-Black-CPU interaction.
Castling and en passant are optional follow-up polish; do not block the base playable clone on them.

compositionRecipes.name: Native Piano
assemblyNotes: Use PianoEngine for note metadata and active note state.
Use generated AVAudioEngine/NSSound glue or simple system tones to play PianoNote.frequency; do not hardcode note math outside the copied component.

compositionRecipes.name: Native SpriteKit Physics Game
assemblyNotes: MANDATORY: copy the canonical SpriteKitPhysicsHelpers.swift VERBATIM into Sources/<TargetName>/MChatAIComponents/SpriteKitPhysicsHelpers.swift (preserve the BEGIN/END markers). Then COMPOSE it from app-specific code by calling at least one of: SKPhysicsHelpers.makeCircularBody, SKPhysicsHelpers.makeRectangularBody, SKPhysicsHelpers.makeOvalBody, SKPhysicsHelpers.makeStaticBody, SKPhysicsHelpers.makeSceneBoundary, SKPhysicsHelpers.snapshot(of:), or reference SKArcadeCategory.<name> / SKPhysicsContactSnapshot. Copying without composing fails the Lego provenance gate.

Use SKPhysicsHelpers.lunarThrustForce / .earthThrustForce as your `applyForce` magnitude — DO NOT pick small numbers like 22 or 30. Those are too weak. The presets are tuned so the user has clear vertical authority over gravity even when the body is tilted (cos(zRotation) cuts the y-component of thrust). Real Lunar Lander shipped with mag=22 and the user couldn't fight gravity even at full thrust at 73° tilt.

Replace EVERY raw `SKPhysicsBody(...)` call in your scene code with the corresponding `SKPhysicsHelpers.make<Shape>Body(...)` factory. Don't keep half-and-half — the gate scans for SKPhysicsHelpers usage as the composition signal.

SKPhysicsBody(ellipseOf:) DOES NOT EXIST. Use SKPhysicsHelpers.makeCircularBody for round entities (knights, ghosts, marbles), makeRectangularBody for boxy entities (bricks, ships, landers), or makeOvalBody (which internally uses polygonFrom: with an ellipse CGPath) only when an oval body is genuinely required.

In nonisolated SKPhysicsContactDelegate.didBegin(_ contact:), call SKPhysicsHelpers.snapshot(of: contact) BEFORE entering Task { @MainActor in ... }. SKPhysicsContact itself is task-isolated and cannot cross the actor boundary; the snapshot captures sendable node references and category bitmasks. Reference SKPhysicsContactSnapshot in the @MainActor closure.

Use SKArcadeCategory.player1 / .player2 / .enemy / .projectile / .platform / .lava / .pickup / .goal / .wall as default bitmask values when assigning categoryBitMask. Reference at least one SKArcadeCategory member directly to satisfy the composition signal.
Set physicsWorld.contactDelegate = self in didMove(to:) and physicsWorld.gravity = SKPhysicsHelpers.standardGravity (or .lunarGravity for floaty games).

App-specific glue (scene setup, sprite drawing, control input handling, HUD, command menu) goes in separate files (GameScene.swift, ContentView.swift, App.swift). Do NOT define a local replacement type called SKPhysicsHelpers — that defeats the purpose of the catalog and fails the gate.

If you wire NotificationCenter restart events (e.g. NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(forName: .restart, ...)) in your SKScene, USE the RestartObserverToken helper from SpriteKitPhysicsHelpers.swift and tear down in `override func willMove(from view: SKView)` — NEVER `deinit`. Swift 6 strict concurrency rejects `removeObserver(token)` from deinit because NSObjectProtocol? is non-Sendable (compile error: 'cannot access property X with a non-Sendable type (any NSObjectProtocol)? from nonisolated deinit'). See wisdom rule mac-026.

goldenAssemblies.name: Native Music Piano Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Minimal SwiftUI piano keyboard. PianoEngine drives note metadata + active-note state; click + computer-keyboard input (A W S E D F T G Y H U J) trigger noteOn/noteOff. NSSound.beep() is the placeholder synth — adapt to AVAudioEngine, AudioKit, or AudioUnit at the call site.
notes: Treat example files as glue patterns, not components. Adapt visual style (key colors, octave layout, sustain pedal UI), audio backend (AVAudioEngine, AudioKit, AudioUnit), and keyboard mapping to the user's requested product. The patterns to KEEP are: (a) PianoEngine as the source of truth for note metadata + active-note state, (b) tap + computer-keyboard input → engine.noteOn / engine.noteOff, (c) UI highlight bound to engine.activeNotes.contains(note.id).
PianoEngine is a byte-for-byte canonical copy under Sources/<TargetName>/MChatAIComponents/. Do NOT redefine PianoNote.frequency or override the MIDI math.

For multi-octave keyboards: instantiate multiple PianoEngine(octave:) — keep them in an array and concatenate notes for rendering. The engine is intentionally per-octave so noteOn/noteOff scoping stays simple.
For real audio: replace NSSound.beep() in ContentView.strike with an AVAudioEngine sine-wave node tuned to PianoNote.frequency, OR a sampled-piano AUSampler patch.

goldenAssemblies.name: Native SpriteKit Physics Game Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Minimal Lunar-Lander-flavored SpriteKit reference: SwiftUI host with SpriteView, GameModel observable, GameScene with SKPhysicsContactDelegate, and a visible game-over restart button (mac-022). Demonstrates the canonical wiring of SpriteKitPhysicsHelpers — gravity preset, makeRectangularBody/makeStaticBody/makeSceneBoundary factories, lunarThrustForce preset for applyForce, and the snapshot(of:)+@MainActor Task pattern for didBegin contact handling.
notes: Treat example files as glue patterns, not components. Adapt visual style, sprite art, terrain shapes, control mappings, scoring, and gameplay rules to the user's requested product. The patterns to KEEP are: (a) physicsWorld.contactDelegate wiring, (b) SKPhysicsHelpers factory usage everywhere instead of raw SKPhysicsBody init calls, (c) lunarThrustForce / earthThrustForce presets in applyForce magnitudes, (d) nonisolated didBegin → snapshot(of:) → Task { @MainActor in ... }, (e) game-over overlay with VISIBLE 'Play Again' button (mac-022).
SpriteKitPhysicsHelpers.swift remains a byte-for-byte canonical copy under Sources/<TargetName>/MChatAIComponents/ — do NOT rewrite or rename it.
For Joust-style games, swap lunarGravity for standardGravity and earthThrustForce for the higher per-flap impulse. Keep the makeSceneBoundary edge-loop so mounts can't fly off-screen.

For Marble Madness, replace the lander's rectangular body with makeCircularBody, set allowsRotation = true, and let gravity + tilt drive the marble. Goal becomes the level exit, lava becomes hole-edges via makeStaticBody with category .lava.
Cmd+N keyboard shortcut + on-screen restart button are BOTH required (mac-022 is the on-screen rule; menu shortcut is the keyboard escape hatch).

goldenAssemblies.name: Native Arcade Adventure Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Atari-Adventure-style top-down maze adventure: hero on a tile grid, treasure / keys / enemies, exit tile to win. Driven by GridAdventureEngine.adventureCastleMap() seed. Visible Play Again button on win/loss (mac-022).
notes: Treat example files as glue patterns, not components. Adapt visual style, sprite art, level layout, sound to the user's requested product. The patterns to KEEP are: (a) GridAdventureEngine seeded via adventureCastleMap() (or your own custom map seed), (b) arrow keys → engine.moveHero(.up/.down/.left/.right), (c) Canvas-based grid renderer iterating engine.map and engine.actors, (d) game-over overlay with VISIBLE 'Play Again' button (mac-022).

GridAdventureEngine + ArcadeCore.swift + GridPuzzleCore.swift are byte-for-byte canonical copies under Sources/<TargetName>/MChatAIComponents/. Do NOT rewrite tile types, actor kinds, or the moveHero / stepTraffic logic.

PuzzlePoint uses (row, col) — NOT (column, row). PuzzleGrid uses (rows, columns). The mismatch is by design: points are addressed (row, col) for matrix-style code; grid metadata is exposed as (rows, columns) for readability. Keep the constructor calls in sync.

For a Frogger-style game, swap the seed for froggerLaneMap() and use stepTraffic() in the per-frame timer. For a Pac-Man-style game, swap to mazePelletMap() / pacmanArcadeMap() — both share GridAdventureEngine.

goldenAssemblies.name: Native Platformer Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Minimal SwiftUI Canvas-based platformer with PlatformerEngine driving player movement, gravity, jumping, and platform collision. Includes a visible Play Again restart button on game-over (mac-022) and Cmd+N CommandMenu shortcut.
notes: Treat example files as glue patterns, not components. Adapt visual style, sprite art, level layouts, sound, and tuning to the user's requested product. The patterns to KEEP are: (a) PlatformerEngine driving player + platforms + gravity, (b) ArcadeControls feeding engine.update via per-frame timer, (c) game-over overlay with VISIBLE 'Play Again' button (mac-022), (d) Cmd+N CommandMenu shortcut posting Notification.platformerNewGame.

PlatformerEngine + ArcadeCore.swift are byte-for-byte canonical copies under Sources/<TargetName>/MChatAIComponents/ — do NOT rewrite player physics, jump impulse, gravity, or platform-resolve logic. If you need different tuning, surface it as input to the engine, not a fork.

For BurgerTime / Donkey Kong / Mario Bros style games, replace the scene's `coins` with collectibles + add enemy bodies that chase the player (use ArcadeBody.intersects for circle-vs-circle hit detection per mac-018). Keep PlatformerEngine.update as the gravity/jump source of truth.
For Pyramid Hopper / Q*bert hops, remove the gravity-fall path and lock the player to discrete diamond cells; PlatformerEngine isn't the right engine for that. Use grid-puzzle.core instead.
